data.title: SA analyst upgrades/downgrades: AMD, ACN, LULU, NFLX
data.description:
Recent analyst actions include Accenture (ACN) receiving an upgrade as bookings rebound and GenAI demand accelerates, while lululemon (LULU) was upgraded to Hold amid CEO transition hopes despite shaky fundamentals. On the downgrade side, AMD (AMD) faces valuation concerns despite strong AI sentiment, and Netflix (NFLX) continues its slide as analysts argue the stock price must adjust to reality.

Upgrades

  • Accenture (ACN): Upgrade Hold to Buy by Redfox Capital Ideas. The analyst upgraded based on a massive bookings rebound, with new bookings up 12% year-over-year to $20.9 billion, and GenAI bookings now exceeding 10% of total bookings.

    “Bookings have recovered nicely, the mix has improved, and GenAI bookings are becoming a more tangible growth driver. With valuation still undemanding relative to the market, I believe the risk-reward has turned positive. … ACN still trades near its 10-year low, at a ~10% discount to the S&P 500, but the operating data has clearly shown a solid recovery.”

  • lululemon athletica (LULU): Upgrade Sell to Hold by Bay Area Ideas. The analyst sees improved risk/reward due to upcoming CEO transition and aggressive international expansion plans, despite continued fundamental weakness.

    “While lululemon’s fundamentals are currently weaker than desired, there are some good reasons for investors to be hopeful. … A CEO transition is not an easy thing per se, and some headwinds like tariffs will be hard to mitigate in the near term. Therefore, a cautious stance right now will likely pay off, and I believe investors should avoid jumping the gun.”

Downgrades

  • Advanced Micro Devices (AMD): Downgrade Buy to Hold by Oliver Rodzianko. The analyst cites extreme market valuations and warns that the AI capex cycle is near peak sentiment, suggesting now is the time to trim positions rather than add exposure.

    “When I look at AMD, I see a genuinely remarkable company and CEO. But I also see a crowd of market participants that have projected their value onto the stock to the detriment of the equity’s intrinsic valuation being accurately depicted in public markets. … A consensus rating of +23.5% upside in the next 12 months means nothing in the face of an aggregate market valuation that is more than +2 standard deviations above fair value.”

  • Netflix (NFLX): Downgrade to Sell by Long Player. The analyst argues that despite earnings and cash flow growth, the current valuation implies unsustainable 30%+ annual growth, with potential for 50% more downside.

    “The business is fine. It is the stock price that needs adjusting. Mr. Market is coming out of one of those periods of irrational exuberance that has marked this stock price since it went public. … The latest earnings report continues a growth rate that is expected for a large company like this that is nothing close to that price-earnings ratio.”

data.title: Halliburton says prepared to quickly restart Venezuela operations
data.description:
Halliburton (HAL) +4.6% in Wednesday's trading after reporting Q4 adjusted earnings and revenues that topped analyst expectations and said it is ready to quickly restart operations in Venezuela when it secures U.S. government approval and payment certainty.

Q4 net income slipped to $589M, or $0.70/share, from $615M, also equivalent to $0.70/share, in the year-earlier quarter, and revenues rose less than 1% Y/Y to $5.66B, as sales across the company's completion and production division gained 2.8% to $3.27B, partially offset by a 1.8% sales decline to $2.39B in the drilling and evaluation segment.

CEO Jeff Miller expressed confidence that Halliburton (HAL) could quickly ramp up its business in Venezuela, which currently represents a small market for the company, but the CEO expects it to become a much bigger business over time.

"I think we could scale up fairly quickly" in Venezuela, Miller said on Halliburton's (HAL) earnings conference call, adding the company is working to secure licenses from the Trump administration that allow it to operate in the country.

"My phone is ringing off the hook in terms of interest in Halliburton being there," Miller said on the call. "I think there are opportunities for us sooner rather than later."

Miller said he sees 2026 as "a rebalancing year," with Halliburton (HAL) expecting revenues to decline in the current quarter, guiding for sales to fall 7%-9% Q/Q across its completion and production division - hurt in part by a slowdown in international activity - and sliding 2%-4% Q/Q across its drilling and evaluation unit.

data.title: Supreme Court eyes Fed independence in Trump-Cook fight - report
data.description:
U.S. Supreme Court justices expressed unease over President Donald Trump's attempt to oust Federal Reserve Governor Lisa Cook, according to a Wednesday media report.

Removing a central bank official on unproven claims -- that Cook committed mortgage fraud -- could erode the Fed's independence and shake markets, the justices cautioned, Bloomberg reported. The court was hearing arguments Wednesday over the Trump-Cook case.

The justices pressed US Solicitor General D. John Sauer on his request that the court allow Trump to remove Cook on an interim basis as her lawsuit proceeds.

Justice Brett Kavanaugh, who was appointed by Trump, said the president's position would "weaken if not shatter" Fed independence, Bloomberg reported. 

Meanwhile, Justice Amy Coney Barrett raised the possibility that potential market disruption should give the court pause, while signaling she has yet to fully align herself with Cook's position. She, too, was appointed by Trump.

In a public show of support for Cook, Fed Chair Jerome Powell -- also mired in a legal clash with Trump -- had reportedly planned to attend the oral arguments at the Supreme Court.

Dear readers: We recognize that politics often intersects with the financial news of the day, so we invite you to click here to join the separate political discussion.

data.title: Intel leads chip stocks higher ahead of its latest earnings report
data.description:
The semiconductor sector, along with much of the broader market, was mostly in the green during Wednesday market action, with Intel (INTC) jumping ahead of the release of its fourth-quarter financial results.

Intel shares had climbed 9% by early afternoon trading. The company is slated to release its latest quarterly financial results after the markets close on Thursday.

Micron Technology (MU) was up 5%, AMD (AMD) had increased 5.5%, while Marvell Technology (MRVL) had ticked up 2%. Nvidia (NVDA) had inched up 0.7%. Cirrus Logic (CRUS), GlobalFoundries (GFS) and Onsemi (ON) were all up 3.5%.

The Philadelphia Semiconductor Index (SOX) had increased nearly 2%.

However, Credo Technology (CRDO) had sunk 9%, while Astera Labs (ALAB) had dropped 7.5%.

Heading into earnings season, J.P. Morgan said many semiconductor firms appear to have "considerable upside" to the AI accelerator total addressable market.  

The major Wall Street indices were all in the green on Wednesday as well after U.S. President Donald Trump eased market concerns about a U.S. invasion to secure Greenland during a visit to Davos for the World Economic Forum.

data.title: SmartFinancial targets $4 EPS run rate by end of 2026 as Columbus expansion accelerates
data.description:
Earnings Call Insights: SmartFinancial, Inc. (SMBK) Q4 2025

Management View

  • William Carroll, President and CEO, highlighted another record year for SmartFinancial, emphasizing, "we continue to increase the tangible book value of our company, which is now up to $26.85 per share. That's growth of over 13% annualized quarter-over-quarter and 17% for the year." Carroll stated operating earnings for the quarter were $13.7 million or $0.81 per diluted share, marking the seventh consecutive quarter of positive operating leverage and annual record earnings of over $51 million.
  • Carroll noted, "we posted 13% annualized growth in loans and 8% annualized growth in deposits," and drew attention to total operating revenue of $53.3 million and pre-provision net revenue hitting $20.9 million in Q4, a 44% year-over-year increase.
  • Carroll announced the planned expansion into the Columbus, Georgia market, stating, "Over the last couple of weeks, we started the process to expand this region of our footprint. Our style of banking is going to play exceptionally well in Columbus, and we look forward to getting ramped up in 2026."
  • Ronald Gorczynski, EVP & CFO, reported, "non-broker deposits growing by $214 million, nearly 18% annualized from both new deposit production...cost of 2.60% which was down 87 basis points from the prior quarter and from seasonal inflows." Gorczynski added, "interest-bearing deposit costs declined by 19 basis points to 2.79%...paid down $112 million in broker deposits...anticipate paying down an additional $44 million during Q1."
  • Gorczynski stated, "net interest margin increased by 13 basis points to 3.38%." He projected Q1 2026 margin in the 3.4% to 3.45% range and noted provision expense was $4.1 million, with $2.4 million allocated to the fountain equipment subsidiary. He also noted, "operating noninterest income reached $8.2 million, surpassing our expectations due to elevated mortgage banking revenue and customer swap fees."

Outlook

  • Carroll reaffirmed, "we feel very confident in our ability to move through to 1% and 12% ROA and ROE targets we achieved in '25 as we look into 2026."
  • Carroll set a new internal goal: "We set a challenge goal to hit a $4 EPS run rate by the end of '26, so basically hitting $1 in earnings per share by Q4."
  • Gorczynski projected first quarter 2026 margin in the 3.4% to 3.45% range and forecasted noninterest income to be approximately $7.6 million and noninterest expense in the $33.5 million to $34 million range.
  • Salary and benefit expenses are anticipated at $20.5 million to $21 million for Q1 due to seasonality and new hires.

Financial Results

  • Carroll reported, "total operating revenue came in at $53.3 million" for the quarter, with pre-provision net revenue rising to $20.9 million.
  • Operating noninterest expenses were flat to Q3 at $32.5 million, and the operating efficiency ratio improved to 60% from 64% last quarter.
  • Gorczynski noted a quarter-end loan-to-deposit ratio of 85% and stated the company remains well capitalized, with a total consolidated risk-based capital at 12.67% and tangible common equity ratio at 7.9%.
  • Nonperforming assets were at 0.22% of total assets, and 2025 net charge-offs to average loans were 8 basis points.

Q&A

  • Russell Elliott Gunther, Stephens Inc.: Asked about sustainability of double-digit loan growth for 2026 and recruitment pipeline. Carroll responded, "we're targeting...high-single-digit plus. That means there might be some quarters where we exceed 10%. But I think if we can hang in there to that 8% to 9-ish, I think that helps us get to where we want to in '26."
  • Gunther requested expense growth guidance. Gorczynski answered, "We're expecting to stay within the $34.5 million to $35 million band. So we're probably targeting around 5% overall expense growth year-over-year."
  • Catherine Mealor, KBW: Inquired about NIM expansion and its role in hitting the $1 per share run rate. Gorczynski replied, "After the first quarter, 3.40%, 3.45% range, we're now looking probably to stay at 3.45%. I would see it probably getting to the 3.50%, plus or minus range by year-end."
  • Stephen Moss, Raymond James: Asked about deposit funding costs and Columbus hiring impact. Gorczynski stated, "we intend to go down probably around, I'd say, 17, 18 basis points to Q1." Carroll added, "we make sure that we kind of balance the expense growth with production...I don't think you'll see a material impact in even as we hire."
  • Stephen Scouten, Piper Sandler: Asked about potential new expansion markets. Carroll said, "We've got tons of opportunity, especially in markets like Nashville and in markets like Birmingham...But nothing really that looks like Columbus on our Board today."

Sentiment Analysis

  • Analysts expressed slightly positive sentiment, focusing on sustainability of growth, margin expansion, and expense control. Repeated interest in guidance clarity and market expansion indicated confidence but also a desire for precision.
  • Management maintained a confident and upbeat tone, frequently reiterating strong execution and disciplined growth. Carroll emphasized, "Just stay on the path, stay diligent in our process, keep grinding."
  • Compared to the previous quarter, analyst tone was consistent, with ongoing questions about organic growth and margin, while management's tone remained optimistic and focused on operational execution.

Quarter-over-Quarter Comparison

  • Guidance shifted from a focus on 10%+ growth to high-single-digit plus for loan growth in 2026.
  • Strategic focus expanded with the announced Columbus, Georgia market entry, in contrast to last quarter's broader regional growth efforts.
  • Efficiency ratio improved from 64% to 60%, and the tangible book value per share increased from $26 to $26.85.
  • Management's confidence in achieving new financial targets, notably the $4 EPS run rate by end of 2026, was more explicitly articulated.
  • Analysts continued to probe for details on margin expansion, expense growth, and hiring, similar to prior quarters.

Risks and Concerns

  • Gorczynski highlighted continued isolated challenges in the fountain equipment loan portfolio, stating $2.4 million of provision was allocated there.
  • Management noted the transition to a new allowance model, which could slightly increase the allowance for credit losses ratio.
  • Carroll and Gorczynski outlined the importance of balancing new hires and expansion with expense discipline.

Final Takeaway

SmartFinancial capped 2025 with record earnings, robust organic growth, and an improved efficiency ratio, driven by disciplined expense management and a focus on core deposit and loan growth. Management remains confident in sustaining high-single-digit loan growth, expanding into Columbus, Georgia, and targeting a $4 EPS run rate by the end of 2026, while maintaining a prudent approach to capital allocation and risk management. The company is positioned for continued operational momentum and earnings expansion as it enters the new year.

Read the full Earnings Call Transcript

data.title: Wall Street moves higher after Trump speaks at Davos
data.description:
Wall Street trades higher on Wednesday after President Donald Trump eased market concerns about a U.S. invasion to secure Greenland.

The tech-focused Nasdaq Composite (COMP:IND) was last higher by 0.1%. At the same time, the benchmark S&P 500 (SP500) was up by 0.3%, and the blue-chip Dow (DJI) had gained 0.4%. 

From a sector vantage point, seven of the S&P 500’s 11 sectors were in positive trading territory with energy leading the way. In reverse, consumer staples has been the weakest performer. 

Treasury yields were mostly even with the shorter-end U.S. 2-year Treasury yield (US2Y) moved down 1 basis point to 3.58%, while the U.S. 10-year Treasury yield (US10Y) was near flat at 4.28%. 

Trump said at the WEF in Davos that he was seeking immediate negotiations for the acquisition of Greenland from Denmark and doesn't want to use excessive force. 

“Stocks are reacting to tariff fears for the first time in months, which is especially notable since many market participants thought stocks had become desensitized to tariff fears. We remind investors that tariff headlines can cause short-term volatility, but that applies in both directions. The tariff threats can easily be unwound and reversed, sparking upside market volatility,” David Laut, chief investment officer at Kerux Financial stated. 

Top S&P 500 gainers included Intel (INTC) +7.6% and United Airlines (UAL) +3.2%. Decliners included Kraft Heinz (KHC) -5.3%, Netflix (NFLX) -3%.

data.title: Apellis upgraded at BofA on launch trajectory for kidney disease therapy
data.description:
Bank of America upgraded Apellis Pharmaceuticals (APLS) to Buy from Neutral on Wednesday, noting that the company’s current valuation doesn’t reflect the strong commercial rollout of its kidney disease therapy, Empaveli.

Analyst Tazeen Ahmad argued that the company has recorded 267 new patient start forms for Empaveli since its approval by the FDA in July for C3 glomerulopathy (C3G) or primary immune complex membranoproliferative glomerulonephritis (IC-MPGN).

She wrote, “Management reports strong early payer progress, with no full denials to date, and attributes the rapid uptake to Empaveli’s broader label and superior efficacy versus competitor Fabhalta,” from Novartis (NVS).

Ahmad added that Apellis’ (APLS) partner Sobi (BIOVF) (SWOBY) has also received EU approval for the drug, also known as pegcetacoplan, against C3G and IC-MPGN recently.

With her $28 per share target unchanged, she argued that BofA is encouraged by the strong launch trajectory, and there’s “room for continued upside as momentum builds in coming quarters.”

data.title: OpenAI pushes global initiative to increase AI usage in daily life
data.description:
OpenAI (OPENAI) believes it can help level the global playing field in the capabilities of governments and their citizens by integrating artificial intelligence tools into an array of daily activities.

The Sam Altman-led company contends nations can use these tools to "raise productivity, strengthen institutions, and expand opportunity." This includes the realms of education, health, AI skills training and certifications, disaster response and preparedness, cybersecurity and start-up accelerators. 

It's an initiative called OpenAI for Countries, which the Microsoft-backed (MSFT) company introduced last year. On Wednesday, OpenAI expanded this initiative with OpenAI's Education for Countries program. 

"It's designed to help governments bring AI into their education systems in ways that strengthen learning and prepare students for the jobs of the future—and to work hand-in-hand with governments to improve our models and education tools," OpenAI said.

OpenAI already has a slate of partner nations for its education program, including Estonia, the United Arab Emirates, Greece, Jordan, Slovakia, Kazakhstan, Trinidad & Tobago, and Italy's CRUI.

OpenAI said that many of its users, even paying subscribers, are not utilizing the higher-capability tools that can increase the thinking power of an individual. 

"Even among monthly active paying ChatGPT Enterprise users, 19% have never used data analysis, 14% have never used reasoning, and 12% have never used search," OpenAI said in a report published today. "This is despite popular benchmarks that demonstrate AI's proven capabilities to reliably solve a meaningful share of common knowledge worker tasks."

"Major economies like the United States, India and Brazil lead in top users, and smaller, higher-income countries like Singapore and the Netherlands lead in population penetration," the report noted. "However, Pakistan, Morocco, and Vietnam have the highest average usage of advanced thinking capabilities, outpacing many wealthier economies."

OpenAI for Countries is trying to encourage more users to embrace these advanced tools and evolve beyond simply using its basic chat feature. OpenAI already has 11 partners for this program, including Argentina, Australia, Estonia, Germany, Greece, Ireland, Italy, Norway, South Korea, the U.A.E., and the U.K.

"In the months ahead, OpenAI for Countries will continue expanding these partnerships—working with governments, educators, entrepreneurs, and public institutions to turn AI into practical capability across healthcare and education systems, workforces, public services, and other essential national functions," OpenAI said. "They will help narrow the capability overhang and put AI into the hands of more people."

data.title: Travelers signals $3.3B fixed income NII outlook for 2026 as AI investments reshape operations
data.description:
Earnings Call Insights: The Travelers Companies, Inc. (TRV) Q4 2025

Management View

  • CEO Alan Schnitzer reported, "We're pleased to report excellent fourth quarter and full year results, with strong and broad-based performance across both underwriting and investments. For both periods, the bottom line results were driven by very strong underlying underwriting income. Particularly given the written margins remain attractive, this is a durable dynamic."
  • Schnitzer highlighted that core income for the quarter was $2.5 billion or $11.13 per diluted share, with a core return on equity of 29.6%. Underwriting income rose 21% compared to the prior year quarter, aided by higher favorable prior year reserve development and lower catastrophe losses.
  • Schnitzer noted, "Through disciplined marketplace execution across all 3 segments, we grew net written premiums to $10.9 billion in the quarter. In Business Insurance, we grew net written premiums to $5.5 billion. Excluding the property line, we grew domestic net written premiums in the segment by 4%."
  • Schnitzer described the company's ongoing transformation, emphasizing, "Now we're bringing all that hard one know-how to Innovation 2.0 at Travelers, powered by AI and not too far off quantum computing."
  • Schnitzer announced a partnership with Anthropic: "Last week, we and Anthropic announced a partnership to empower 10,000 of our engineers, data scientists, analysts and product owners with personalized context-aware and integrated AI assistance."
  • Schnitzer stated, "In our claim organization, more than half of all claims are now eligible for straight-through processing, with customers adopting straight-through processing about 2/3 of the time."
  • CFO Daniel Frey stated, "Core income for the fourth quarter was $2.5 billion and core return on equity was 29.6%, as we once again delivered excellent financial results on a consolidated basis and in all 3 segments."
  • Frey shared, "At 82.2%, the underlying combined ratio marked its fifth consecutive quarter below 85%. The combination of higher premiums and the improved underlying combined ratio led to a 15% increase in after-tax underlying underwriting income."

Outlook

  • Frey reported, "We continue to expect the expense ratio for 2026 to be right around 28.5%."
  • Frey provided investment guidance: "In terms of our outlook for fixed income NII for 2026, including earnings from short-term securities, we expect approximately $3.3 billion after tax, beginning with about $800 million in the first quarter and growing to about $870 million in the fourth quarter."
  • The company expects to execute around $1.8 billion of share repurchases in Q1 2026, with actual amount dependent on factors like cat events and quarterly earnings.
  • Schnitzer stated, "Operating from this position of strength, we remain highly confident in the outlook for Travelers in 2026 and beyond."

Financial Results

  • Schnitzer reported net written premiums of $10.9 billion for the quarter.
  • Frey stated, "During 2025, we grew our investment portfolio by approximately $7.5 billion to $106 billion."
  • Catastrophe losses for the quarter were $95 million pretax.
  • Total net favorable reserve development was $321 million pretax in the quarter, with all three segments contributing.
  • Frey noted, "After-tax net investment income of $867 million increased by 10% from the prior year quarter."
  • Adjusted book value per share was $158.01 at year-end, up 14% from a year ago.
  • The company returned $1.9 billion of capital to shareholders in the quarter, including $1.65 billion of share repurchases.

Q&A

  • Charles Peters, Raymond James: Asked about the timeline for material expense ratio improvement and regulatory considerations impacting tech investments. Schnitzer responded, "We want the gains from the efficiencies that we're generating. And that just gives us a lot of flexibility in the way we run the business... we can let it fall to the bottom line if we want through lower expense ratio."
  • Peters, Raymond James: Asked about regulatory focus on personal lines profitability. Schnitzer stated, "This really is a business that you need to look at and manage over a period of time... when you think about Personal Insurance results over a period of time, I mean, certainly, in our case, you wouldn't say that we're over-earning."
  • Ryan Tunis, Cantor Fitzgerald: Asked about the deceleration in renewal premium change ex property and property rate adequacy. Schnitzer and Greg Toczydlowski attributed it to "a little bit of both" rate and exposure, and Schnitzer said, "It's not so crazy when you think about where the returns are."
  • David Motemaden, Evercore: Asked about buyback outlook and catastrophe loss expectations. Frey clarified, "There's no change in our capital management strategy... the rest of the year is going to be impacted by all the usual things that would impact buybacks."
  • Michael Zaremski, BMO: Asked about headcount trends from tech initiatives. Schnitzer stated, "Premium per employee is up, thanks to some productivity and efficiency initiatives, and we expect premium per employee to continue to go up."
  • Katie Sakys, Autonomous Research: Asked about durability of underlying underwriting results. Schnitzer said, "We are very confident in our ability to continue writing premium at substantial levels."

Sentiment Analysis

  • Analysts raised recurring concerns about the timeline for expense ratio improvement, regulatory pressures, rate adequacy, buyback sustainability, and the competitive landscape, with a slightly positive to neutral tone, seeking clarity on strategic execution and risk management.
  • Management remained confident but cautiously acknowledged uncertainty, frequently emphasizing flexibility, discipline, and the durability of earnings, as reflected in phrases like "we remain highly confident in the outlook for Travelers in 2026 and beyond."
  • Compared to last quarter, analyst sentiment was similarly focused on top-line growth and expense leverage, while management maintained a steady, confident tone in both prepared remarks and Q&A.

Quarter-over-Quarter Comparison

  • Guidance for the expense ratio remains unchanged at 28.5% for 2026, consistent with Q3 commentary.
  • Strategic focus intensified on AI-driven operational improvements, with new disclosures about generative AI deployment and a partnership with Anthropic in Q4, compared to a broader focus on technology and data in Q3.
  • Analysts in both quarters questioned the pace of expense ratio leverage and capital deployment, but Q4 brought increased discussion around the structural impact of technology investments.
  • Net written premiums in Q4 ($10.9 billion) were slightly lower than Q3 ($11.5 billion), and underwriting income and investment income reached new highs in Q4.
  • Management's confidence in the durability of underwriting and investment results was reiterated, with added emphasis on AI as a transformative factor for operational efficiency and growth.

Risks and Concerns

  • Schnitzer and Frey acknowledged the impact of regulatory scrutiny, the need to balance profitability with affordability, and the potential for catastrophe losses.
  • Frey noted, "The second quarter has historically been our largest cat quarter," highlighting ongoing seasonal risk.
  • Analysts questioned potential drags on growth from reinsurance structures and possible regulatory pushback on personal lines profitability.
  • Management pointed to continued uncertainty in long-tail casualty lines, maintaining an uncertainty provision in the loss ratio for 2026.
  • Schnitzer stated, "We are very confident in our ability to continue writing premium at substantial levels," but acknowledged that results must be managed over time given volatility and regulatory dynamics.

Final Takeaway

The Travelers Companies capped the year with record underwriting and investment results, underpinned by disciplined pricing, strong risk selection, and significant advances in technology and AI integration. Management highlighted continued operational leverage and strategic investments, especially in AI, as catalysts for ongoing efficiency gains and profitability. The company expects to maintain its expense ratio, targets $3.3 billion in fixed income net investment income for 2026, and remains committed to substantial capital returns, while keeping a focused approach to risk, regulatory developments, and long-term shareholder value creation.

Read the full Earnings Call Transcript

data.title: Pan American Silver says 2025 silver output hit record, topped guidance
data.description:
Pan American Silver (PAAS) said late Tuesday it produced a record 7.3M oz of silver in Q4, lifting FY 2025 output to 22.8M oz and exceeding its full-year forecast, and guided for silver production for 2026 of 25M-27M oz.

Pan American (PAAS) also said it produced 197.8K oz of gold during Q4, raising its total to 742.2K oz for FY 2025, within its full-year guidance.

The miner said the Juanicipio mine in Mexico exceeded expectations, contributing 2.5M oz of silver since the mine was acquired in September 2025 and provided $44M in December 2025.

Cash and short-term investments are estimated at nearly $1.32B at year-end 2025, up 45% compared to the end of Q3.

For 2026, Pan American (PAAS) guided for attributable silver production of 25M-27M oz and gold production of 700K-750K oz, with all-in sustaining costs of $15.75-$18.25/oz for the silver segment and $1,700-$1,850/oz for gold.

FY 2026 capital spending is forecast at $515M-$550M, consisting of $320M-$340M of sustaining capital and $195M-$210M of project capital, including a new phased development approach for the La Colorada Skarn and vein mine.

Pan American (PAAS) shares -1.3% on Wednesday after posting an all-time high $60.25 earlier in the session, as the rally in precious metals futures to all-time highs cooled a bit after President Trump said the U.S. would not use excessive force to obtain Greenland.
